description: Tetsu Kasuya 4:6 method — YouTube transcript extraction
sourceText:
YouTube title:
Tetsu Kasuya's 4:6 Method — The World Champion V60 Recipe

YouTube description:
Learn Tetsu Kasuya's award-winning 4:6 V60 method. This technique won the World Brewers Cup and is known for its simplicity and consistency.

YouTube transcript:
today I'm going to show you the four six method this is a technique developed by Tetsu Kasuya who won the World Brewers Cup in 2016 the idea is really simple you split your total water into 40% and 60% the first 40% controls sweetness and acidity the second 60% controls strength so let's get started we're using 20 grams of coffee ground coarse and 300 grams of water at 93 degrees Celsius the total ratio is 1 to 15 step one pour 60 grams of water and wait until 45 seconds step two pour another 60 grams to reach 120 grams and wait until 1 minute 30 now we move to the second phase step three pour 60 grams to reach 180 grams wait until 2 minutes 15 step four pour 60 grams to reach 240 grams wait until 3 minutes step five pour the final 60 grams to reach 300 grams the total drawdown should finish around 3 minutes 30 to 3 minutes 45 the grind size should be coarser than typical V60 recipes because we're doing multiple pours with longer contact time
